query: London to Brighton Bike Ride gran fondo cycling London to Brighton

videos.title: Can We Ride 88 Kilometres In Under 2 Hours? London To Brighton Record Attempt
videos.description:
The super nerds on super bikes are back. This time we’re trying to ride from London to Brighton in just 2 hours! If we pull it off, it’ll set a new record. This 155-year-old route stretches 55 miles, starting from the capital's bustling streets and ending at England's southern coast in Brighton. Will we make it? 

In collaboration with @volkswagenvans 🤝 https://gcn.eu/VWVans

00:00 How fast can we make it to Brighton?
01:05 Thanks VW Commercial Vehicles For Supporting Us!
01:37 The Route & Event 
02:32 Full gas out of London!
03:39 First Uphill 
04:52 1 Hour In 
07:43 The Strategy
08:37 Conor & Hank Mile 29
09:05 First Obstacle 
12:09 Hank & Conor’s Progress 
14:05 Finish Line  
16:32 How did that go?!

Useful Links:
Join this channel to get access to perks 👉 
https://gcn.eu/JoinYTmemberships
Insurance: Viewers in the USA and UK can explore GCN Insurance products 👉 https://gcn.eu/GCNinsurance
GCN Uploader 👉 https://gcn.eu/gcnuploader
Visit the GCN Shop 👉 https://gcn.eu/gcnshop
GCN WhatsApp Channel  👉 https://gcn.eu/GCNwhatsapp
GCN Instagram Broadcast Channel 👉 https://gcn.eu/instagrambroadcast

What did you think of this ride? 💭 Would you participate in a race like this? 🚴‍♂️💨 If so, would you try to stick in the front and aim for speed or performance, or hang back, enjoy the party and have fun? 

Watch more on GCN...
📹 How Far Can 2 Ex-Pro Cyclists Ride 1 Death Trap Tandem? 👉 https://youtu.be/TutqWw148gA?si=rKyjTarmkDj5-ubI
📹 Watch our Editor’s Choice Playlist 👉 https://gcn.eu/editorschoice
📹 Or why not check out our Features Playlist 👉 ​​https://gcn.eu/gcnfeatures

The Global Cycling Network (GCN) is the largest online cycling channel in the world, bringing together a global community of road cyclists to celebrate everything that’s great about the world of cycling.

Our videos bring fans compelling daily content including expert tutorials, techniques, training, racing, cutting-edge bike tech, unparalleled behind the scenes event coverage, humour,  entertainment, and more.

Presented by ex-pro riders, GCN offers a uniquely qualified insight into the world of cycling, and most importantly it’s fuelled by our passionate and enthusiastic fans – everyone who makes up the GCN community. We also bring the latest and greatest tech to your attention, showcase the best places in the world to ride and get exclusive access to events and races.

videos.title: London to Brighton Bike Ride Finish | Sussex Gravel Ride
videos.description:
With a bit of hangover I meet up with Jason & Vicky for a Sussex gravel ride. After coffee and rain we split up. I then go and watch the finish of the London to Brighton Bike Ride.

This channel is supported by - https://beaconbikes.co.uk

IG - https://www.instagram.com/the_muddy_cyclist/
Strava - https://www.strava.com/athletes/32671435
FB - https://www.facebook.com/themuddycyclist
videos.transcript:

videos.title: British Heart Foundation - Make a difference with the BHF London to Brighton Bike Ride
videos.description:
https://www.bhf.org.uk/l2b

Thanks to everyone who took part in 2010 BHF London to Brighton Bike Ride. All funds raised go towards the fight against heart disease.

Registration is open for next year's ride, but why wait? Please check out http://www.bhf.org.uk/events and get involved now.
videos.transcript:
[Music]
eating heart disease
together the reason I chose to take part
today is that my young brother is about
to have a heart transplant and um as a
family we're all quite upset by it all
and then we thought you know what can we
do my daughter suggested that we uh do
the London toe um Brighton bike ride to
raise money and we thought oh yeah
that'd be a great idea we did it and
we're very very pleased that we did it
but we didn't realize how many people
are affected by heart disease and you
think well yeah I ought to really do
something El an awful lot of people who
take part in this have got some
particular reason for wanting to raise
money for the fight against heart
disease but also because it's just such
huge fun it's just a wonderful wonderful
party atmosphere best Char I've ever
been on and we've been on a few haven't
we we have yes it's a really good
atmosphere good atmosphere atmosphere is
brilliant yeah um recommend it to
anybody
my father died of a heart attack about
19 months ago and with it being Father's
Day and the London to Brighton bike ride
we uh felt it was a good way of spending
time together and enjoying the day and
helping to raise money for the
[Applause]
charity if you're somebody who wants a
good reason to get out and cycle and you
need a bit of motivation um and you want
to raise money for a good cause it's a
great thing to look forward to um so i'
really encourage you to do it and the
sense of achievement is unbelievable

videos.title: Can We Do An Epic Ride Around London?
videos.description:
London is one of the world’s biggest and most populous cities but certainly not the first place you’d think about when planning the perfect ride. But should it be? Hank met with London native Alec Briggs for a tour of the UK’s capital to find out if you can do an epic ride in a big city!

Watch Cycling Heartlands: London on GCN+ 👉 https://gcn.eu/HeartlandsLDN
Check out the route on Komoot 👉 https://gcn.eu/LondonRoute

Useful Links:
Adventure Documentaries, Exclusive Shows & Live Racing on GCN+: https://gcn.eu/plus
Download the GCN App for free: https://gcn.eu/app
Visit the GCN Shop: https://gcn.eu/gcnshop
Join the GCN Club: https://www.gcnclub.com/

Where would you choose to go for the perfect city ride? Let us know in the comments below! 💬

If you liked this video, be sure to give it a thumbs up 👍 and share it with your friends!

Watch more on GCN...
📹 Italy’s Ultimate Bike Ride - GCN’s Dolomite Epic 👉 https://youtu.be/CYTEOrmUzPo

The Global Cycling Network (GCN) is the largest online cycling channel in the world, bringing together a global community of road cyclists to celebrate everything that’s great about the world of cycling.

Our videos bring fans compelling daily content including expert tutorials, techniques, training, racing, cutting-edge bike tech, unparalleled behind the scenes event coverage, humour,  entertainment, and more.

Presented by ex-pro riders, GCN offers a uniquely qualified insight into the world of cycling, and most importantly it’s fuelled by our passionate and enthusiastic fans – everyone who makes up the GCN community. We also bring the latest and greatest tech to your attention, showcase the best places in the world to ride and get exclusive access to events and races.

videos.title: Chain Gang Cyclists London To Brighton Back To London For Mind Charity
videos.description:
Sunday morning at 6:15am Chain Gang Cyclists met at Richmond Park to do London to Brighton & back to London. Only 2 riders in the group of more than 30 cyclists both male and female of varying ability had completed this mammoth challenge.

On this day Chain Gang Cyclists did something some can only dream of doing, by cycling from London to Brighton & Back to raise money for Mind Charity. Our initial target of £2500 was exceeded on the day the JustGiving page was set up. So we decided to raise the bar. You kind souls made sure we raised it again and again before we set off with a final goal of £7000 which we exceeded by 18% thanks to your generosity. 

Armed with gels water bottles and your support we set off for Brighton. What transpired I can only describe as EPIC! Beautiful people, amazing banter, gorgeous scenery made for a memorable day. Every rider made it to Brighton and the CGC family all made it back to London. We would like to thank those of you that joined us on the day. Some of you cycled down with us one way and others met us in Brighton. Your support was heart felt as it showed how much we are loved and valued. The love is real! 

https://www.justgiving.com/fundraisin...

Chain Gang Cyclists (CGC) was founded during Quarantine 2020. Individuals had developed a passion for cycling as a way to keep fit and get much needed head space during phase 1 lockdown. As restrictions eased several friends who had developed the same love for the tarmac grew together to form a bike chain, which started as a group meet up and has since turned into a weekly occurrence of rides across London and surrounded areas.

Commonly amongst existing and new members we’ve all found that maintaining a positive mindset is so important to a balanced and healthy lifestyle. Our chain rides have really helped to reduce anxiety and remove the worry of the current climate by providing an inclusive environment where members can just ride on the open road and let the journey take you.

Our mission is to encourage other males & females to pursue cycling for self care and improved well being.

Donating through JustGiving is simple, fast and totally secure.

https://www.justgiving.com/fundraisin...
